menu.theoryDesc: 30 multiple-choice questions · 20 minutes · Need 27/30 to pass
menu.practicalDesc: Closed course maneuvers · Parking, serpentine, hill start
menu.cityDesc: Drive through a real Georgian city · Obey all traffic rules

citySelect.title: Select a City
citySelect.subtitle: Choose a city for your exam

rustavi.name: Rustavi
rustavi.desc: Soviet-era grid streets with wide avenues and industrial zones.

telavi.name: Telavi
telavi.desc: Historic wine-country town with fortress walls and vineyard landscapes.

kutaisi.name: Kutaisi
kutaisi.desc: Second largest city — complex intersections and Rioni River crossings.

batumi.name: Batumi
batumi.desc: Coastal city with a famous boulevard, palm trees, and heavy tourist traffic.

akhaltsikhe.name: Akhaltsikhe
akhaltsikhe.desc: Medieval castle town with narrow old streets and steep inclines.

zugdidi.name: Zugdidi
zugdidi.desc: Green city with Dadiani Palace and a well-structured grid layout.

gori.name: Gori
gori.desc: Wide Soviet-era boulevards and the famous Gori Fortress on the hill.

poti.name: Poti
poti.desc: Port city with cranes, cargo ships, and coastal weather effects.

ozurgeti.name: Ozurgeti
ozurgeti.desc: Peaceful Gurian town with a clean grid layout — perfect for learners.

sachkhere.name: Sachkhere
sachkhere.desc: Mountain town with winding roads and dramatic gorge scenery.

ambrolauri.name: Ambrolauri
ambrolauri.desc: Scenic Racha region capital surrounded by vineyards and Caucasus peaks.

akhalkalaki.name: Akhalkalaki
akhalkalaki.desc: Highland border city on the Javakheti plateau with unique architecture.

tips: At a roundabout, vehicles already inside have priority.
Always signal before turning or changing lanes.
The minimum following distance at 60 km/h is 30 meters.
You must stop completely at a STOP sign, even if the road is clear.
Pedestrians always have priority at zebra crossings.
Speed limit in residential areas is 20 km/h unless signed otherwise.
Headlights must be on at all times when driving outside city limits.
A solid white line must never be crossed.
Emergency vehicles with sirens active always have right of way.
